en: I'm very strong.
I'm as strong as you.
I'm stronger than them.
I'm the strongest student in my class.

tr: Çok iyi bir günün ardından evdeyim.
en: I'm at home after a very good day.
I'm as good as you at basketball.

tr: Daha iyi bir gelecek için İngilizce öğrenmek istiyorum.
en: I want to learn English for a better future.
You are the best friend in the world.

tr: Onların evi bir futbol sahası kadar büyük.
en: Their house is as big as a football field.
Our house is bigger than yours.
This is the biggest problem in the world.
The life is as short as a day.

tr: Senin telefonun benimki kadar eski mi?
en: Is your phone as old as mine?
Is your phone old?

tr: Senin telefonun benimkinden daha eski mi?
en: Is your phone older than mine?

tr: Seninki masadaki en eski telefon mu?
en: Is yours the oldest phone on the table?
This car is very expensive.

tr: Bu araba, bir ev kadar pahalı.
en: This car is as expensive as a house.

tr: Bu araba, bir evden daha pahalı.
en: This car is more expensive than a house.
This is the most expensive car in Turkey.
Ankara is very cold in the winters.
Ankara is as cold as Siberia in the winters.
Siberia is colder than Ankara in the winters.

tr: Kışları dünyadaki en soğuk yer Sibirya’dır.
en: The coldest place in the world is Siberia in the winters.
This food is very good.

tr: Bu yemek annemin ki kadar iyi.
en: This food is as good as my mother's.
This food is better than my mother's.

tr: Türkiye’nin en iyi restoranına gitmek istiyoruz.
en: We want to go to the best restaurant of Turkey.
This is very bad.
I'm better.
Are you better?
I'm better than yesterday.
Today is more important than yesterday.
Mount Ararat is very high.
Everest is higher than Mount Ararat.
Everest is the highest mountain in the world.

tr: Ben 30 yıl sonra senin kadar sağlıklı olmak istiyorum.
en: I want to be as healthy as you after 30 years.
Ali is as dangerous as a shark.
Ali is more dangerous than a shark.

tr: Ali bu mahalledeki en tehlikeli adamdır.
en: Ali is the most dangerous man in this neighborhood.

tr: Gömleğim kadar temiz bir hayata başlamak istiyorum.
en: I want to start a life as clean as my shirt.

tr: Senin annen benimki kadar tertipli mi?
en: Is your mother as tidy as mine?

tr: Daha tertipli bir insan ile yaşamak istiyorum.
en: I want to live with a tidier person.

tr: Sen, benim için annem kadar önemlisin.
en: You are as important as my mother for me.

tr: Sen benim için her şeyden daha önemlisin.
en: You are more important than everything for me.

tr: Senin hayatın benimki kadar sıradan mı?
en: Is your life as ordinary as mine?
We're living the most ordinary life in the world.
I'm as happy as a child.
Are you as tired as me?

tr: Ankara'daki trafik, istanbul kadar yoğun değil.
en: The traffic in Ankara isn't as busy as Istanbul.
I'm as angry as a bull.
My clothes are as white as clouds.
My future is as far as my past.

tr: Sen ingilizcede benim kadar iyi misin?
en: Are you as good as me at English?

tr: Sen İngilizcede benden daha mı iyisin?
en: Are you better than me at English?
I want a good life.
I want a better life.
You are my best friend.

tr: Kötü bir günün ardından arkadaşlarımla beraberim.
en: I'm with my friends after a bad day.

tr: Daha kötü bir ev için daha çok para ödüyorum.
en: I pay more money for a worse house.

tr: Daha iyi bir araba için çok param yok.
en: I don't have a lot of money for a better car.
I don't have a lot of money.
I need more money.
I need more time.

tr: Kötü günler için biraz param var.
en: I have some money for bad days.

tr: Daha kötü günler için daha çok paraya ihtiyacım var.
en: I need more money for worse days.
My friend is the best person in the world.
We're working for better days.
I live in a very far village.

tr: Daha uzak bir yerde yaşamak istiyorum.
en: I want to live in a further place.
I don't want to live with less money.

tr: Çocuklarım için daha iyi bir gelecek istiyorum.
en: I want a better future for my children.

tr: Türkiye’deki en iyi arabayı satın almak istiyorum.
en: I want to buy the best car in Turkey.

tr: Daha iyi bir araba satın almak istiyorum.
en: I want to buy a better car.

tr: Çok iyi bir araba satın almak istiyorum.
en: I want to buy a very good car.
This is the best day of my life.
This is the worst day of my life.
You are my best friend in the school.
Our house is further.
I'm stronger.
You are older.
Ali is better.
You are worse.
The weather is hotter.

tr: Sen benden daha iyi bir öğrencisin.
en: You are a better student than me.
I feel better now.
This is the best solution.

tr: Hayatımda daha az sorun ve daha çok para istiyorum.
en: I want less problems and more money in my life.

tr: Dünyadaki en kötü arabalar için çok para vermek istemiyorum.
en: I don't want to give a lot of money for the worst cars in the world.
There are some people on the streets today.

tr: Bugün sokaklarda daha az insan var.
en: There are less people on the streets today.
I don't have more time.

tr: Daha az para için daha çok çalışmak istemiyorum.
en: I don't want to work more for less money.

tr: Daha iyi bir ev için daha çok paraya ihtiyacım var.
en: I need more money for a better house.

tr: Dünyadaki en pahalı arabayı sürmek ister misin?
en: Do you want to drive the most expensive car in the world?
The most expensive houses in Turkey are in Istanbul.
The cheapest breakfast in Istanbul is here.
The coldest city in Turkey is Hakkari.

tr: Dolaptaki en soğuk kolayı içmek istiyorum.
en: I want to drink the coldest cola in the fridge.

tr: Türkiye’nin en sıcak ili Antalya mıdır?
en: Is Antalya the hottest city in Turkey?

tr: Okuldaki en güzel kız Ayşe midir?
en: Is Ayşe the most beautiful girl in the school?

tr: Sınavdaki en kolay sorular bunlar mı?
en: Are these the easiest questions in the exam?
The earliest train is at 9 o'clock.
The latest bus is at 3 o'clock in the morning.
Who is the richest man of Turkey?

tr: Dünyadaki en sağlıklı insanlar Japonya’da yaşıyor.
en: The healthiest people in the world live in Japan.

tr: Bu toplantıda Türkiyenin en önemli sorunları hakkında konuşmak istiyoruz.
en: We want to talk about the most important problems of Turkey in this meeting.

tr: Benim için dünyadaki en önemli şey ailemdir.
en: The most important thing in the world for me is my family.
Ali is my closest friend.
Ayşe is my smartest friend.

tr: Daha ucuz bir kahvaltı için buradayız.
en: We are here for a cheaper breakfast.
Hakkari is colder than Ankara in the winters.
Antalya is hotter than Istanbul in the summers.

tr: Daha soğuk bir ülkede yaşamak istemiyorum.
en: I don't want to live in a colder country.
English is very easy.
English is easier than Turkish.
Turkish is more difficult than English.

tr: Daha kolay bir hayat için Avrupa’ya gitmek istiyorum.
en: I want to go to Europe for a easier life.

tr: Daha güçlü bir ülke için çalışıyoruz.
en: We're working for a stronger country.
Walking in Istanbul is difficult.
Walking in Ankara is more difficult.

tr: İngilizce öğrenmek Türkçe öğrenmekten daha kolaydır.
en: Learning English is easier than learning Turkish.

tr: Senin hayatın benimkinden daha mı önemli?
en: Is your life more important than mine?

tr: İnsanlar, daha konforlu bir yaşam için İstanbul’a geliyorlar.
en: The people come to Istanbul for a more comfortable life.
This movie is more boring than my homework.
My house is closer to the hospital.
Your house is further to the hospital.
I want to sleep earlier.
I want to wake up earlier.
Istanbul is more crowded than Ankara.
Villages are greener than cities.
Nights are darker than days.
